Okay, as Jeff pointed out, there's absolutely no reason not to do it with electronic media at least Because fundamentally once you build the system, there's no cost, right? So why would you stop communicating with them until somebody calls up and says Charlie's dead Right, and if you're really aggressive you say would you mind sending me a copy of the death certificate before we take them off of? Oh Do you guys know the story the guy calling up to try and get off the mailing list you guys know this as a good destructive story old story So a guy calls up it says I want off the list All right, and so the person says well, okay, mr. So-and-so, but I do need to explain something to you right now. You're on the a list Right if I take you off the a list The only thing I'm allowed to do is put you on the b-list Right the problem with being on the b-list is you can never get off the b-list But if you're on the a-list you can get off the a-list when you want to get off the a-list now Are you sure you want me to put you on the b-list or would you rather stay on the a-list? And a confused person says And cuz there's only two choices Okay, this is big switch to a different opportunity of yours So this is we had a conversation earlier red shovel blue shovel, okay? Just because they didn't like finally the shovel you showed them Doesn't mean they didn't want the promise that you made that brought them forward They still want that promise. They just don't like the thing you finally showed them From which they were going to get the promise So they're still a very good prospect in some respects. They may be better because they're all revved up and now disappointed So you need another opportunity that fulfills the same 3 4 5 things that brought them to you that is something else of yours in many cases So like what I just did with Jay Geier so his front-end product is fix the phone. He's got back-end products He's got a referral system product. He's got a case Compliant case presentation product, right? So how the doctor can close more cases when he makes presentations Well either one of those can fulfill the same 4 promises That brought them forward that he tried to sell them fix the staff fix the phone for so Great, let's take the ones that absolutely refuse to buy Fix the staff fix the phone and let's Rekindle the four things and then let's show them case presentation So we move them from opportunity one to opportunity two to opportunity three as many opportunities as you can have Sometimes there's a second tier this is in direct sales So really the only one in the room probably doing is Bill But so you get a bunch so you get a bunch of leads to buy your thing your area your franchise You're very exclusive your distributorship and for one reason or another nor usually they don't have the dough, right? So like in the UPS case if you had a if you had a lesser opportunity Okay, let's say a mobile Pack and ship thing that mounted on a bicycle There was UPS thing and they had a little territory and they went around right and you could put them under the franchisee Right, you can drop them down or you can advertise for that and then move them up. Okay, that's second tier Switch to a different opportunity with a different identity Most people will want to do it, but I will mention it to you anyway because it's big right a Again, you're unconverted leads There's No, there's pretty much only three reasons why they don't buy other than they were just really crappy leads and curiosity And tire kickers to start with okay, there's really only three reasons they don't buy okay a price That is actually or financially insurmountable for them Okay, so they want it, but they can't or Or mom won't let them or they can't emotionally pay that price. So sometimes it's price Sometimes it's the shovel itself. They wanted the promises, but now when we actually show them Got exercise and you got to use this jump rope and you Just like John told me about kettlebell say so he get me all excited about my blood sugar going down and you know And better abs and win races But as soon as he gets to an hour a day lifting this big heavy thing I'm out All right, doesn't mean I don't want to benefits Okay, doesn't mean I won't buy something else, but I am buying that thing All right And but I'm not telling you you shouldn't buy it by the way by all means okay I'm not being disparaging about the thing at all. I'm just being very realistic about what at the moment I'm going to do and I ain't going to lift this big heavy thing that this Russian guy Swings around in with abandon. That's not happening All right, so so the second reason that they don't is they finally don't like they don't like that thing They still want all the promises the third reason they don't is they don't like you So the third reason they don't is they are rejecting they don't believe you they don't like you They're they don't believe your company. They don't like your company Okay, so they don't respond to the personality you put in front of them However, they want those four promises So what big professional Opportunity marketers do is they have another identity with another company in a different place The big skincare marketers do it the big weight loss marketers do it The big religion marketers do it And now they show up Their alter ego shows up Which is different tone different style different backstory Sometimes it's a fake. It's a Betty Crocker. It's a fake alter ego many times It's a different front person often. It's one of their great success stories that they then turn into a parallel business And they own them and sometimes there's three Sometimes there's four
